description_long: This object is a vintage-style record player with a large, circular turntable platter mounted atop a rectangular wooden base. The base appears to be made of wood with a slightly worn, matte finish, and features a hand-crank mechanism on one side, suggesting a manual operation. A prominent, elongated tonearm extends from the side, ending in a flared, horn-like speaker, reminiscent of early gramophones. The tonearm and horn are rendered in a lighter, metallic or painted material, with some visible wear and patina. The platter is broad and flat, with a central spindle, and lacks any visible grooves or records on its surface. There are no visible control knobs, switches, or dust cover, giving the device a simple, utilitarian appearance.
description: A vintage wooden record player with a large flat platter, a hand-crank, and a prominent horn-shaped tonearm. The design is simple, with no visible controls or dust cover.
description_view_0: Front view showing the circular platter, the horn-shaped tonearm extending to the right, and the rectangular wooden base.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the depth of the base, the hand-crank mechanism, and the horn facing forward.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the back of the horn, the hand-crank on the left, and the plain wooden back of the base.
description_view_3: Opposite side view with the horn angled away, showing the side of the platter and the tonearm's attachment point.
attribute_turntable_platter: The turntable platter is present, large, and circular, occupying most of the top surface of the record player. It appears to be made of a smooth, beige material, possibly painted metal or wood, with a central spindle but no visible grooves or patterns. The platter is slightly recessed into the wooden base and has a matte finish.
attribute_tonearm: The tonearm is present, elongated, and extends from the side of the base to above the platter, ending in a large, flared horn. It is relatively thick, appears to be made of metal or painted metal, and has a worn, beige finish. The tonearm is positioned above the platter, angled outward, and features visible joints where it connects to the base and the horn.
